feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP SAC have a data streaming functionality
entry.content:
HI 

Does SAP SAC have a data streaming functionality so that any change of the source table (new or change record) that is replicated in SAP DSP is  automatically pops up in SAP SAC report without manual refresh?

Thanks

entry.title: Buffer table exists with a different structure after structural changes to source table
entry.content:
Hi,

We are getting SAP datasphere replication error "Buffer table exists with a different structure" after we made structural change to a table that is already replicating data.

I have had a look at SAP note 3297105 , and it says that structural changes to source objects are not supported.

Is there a workaround solution in order to avoid doing initialization again.

Thanks,

entry.title: Is there any out bound interface between Datasphere to AWS i.e we want to send data from DS to AWS ?
entry.content: Is there any out bound interface between Datasphere to AWS i.e we want to send data from Datasphere to AWS ?

entry.title: DP Agent installation and configuration issues
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

The situation is described as follows. How should we troubleshoot this issue?

After following the reference documents for installation and configuration, checking the Agent Status reveals that the HANA Connection has not been successfully established. However, the Agents in Datasphere are showing as connected. In this situation, it does not seem possible to register ABAP Adapters with SAP Datasphere.


The version of the HANA DP Agent I installed is:
WINDOWS ON X64 64BIT
SP07 Patch 40 (2.7.4) for HANA DP AGENT 2.0
Operating System: 64-bit Windows Server 2019

framework_alert.trc
2024-06-19 11:57:50,819 [ERROR] DPFramework | RequestHandlerThread.run [] - EOFException : Socket closed while trying to read.
2024-06-19 11:57:55,873 [ERROR] MDSListener | MDSListener.run [] - SocketListener: Listener is shutdown. socket closed
2024-06-19 11:58:00,915 [ERROR] DPFramework | SocketListener.run [] - java.net.SocketException: socket closed
2024-06-19 11:59:30,055 [ERROR] DPFramework | RequestHandlerThread.run [] - Exception for connectorId[127.0.0.1:63821] : java.net.SocketException: Connection reset

agentcli_alert.trc
2024-06-19 11:58:27,827 [ERROR] DPFramework | Message.write [] - Exception while writing to output stream: Connection reset by peer: socket write error (local port 63796 to address 127.0.0.1, remote port 5051 to address 127.0.0.1)
2024-06-19 11:58:31,116 [ERROR] DPFramework | Message.write [] - Exception while writing to output stream: Socket closed

 

Reference documents
p.23
Data Provisioning Agent Installation and Update Guide
Document Version: 1.0 – 2024-05-09

p.40~p.42
Configuration Guide for Other SAP HANA Scenarios
Document Version: 1.0 – 2024-05-09

Connect and Configure the Data Provisioning Agent
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/e87952d7c656477cb5558e5c2f44ae9c.html?locale=en-US

Register Adapters with SAP Datasphere
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/085fc4917f6f4011900344d44d721705.html?locale=en-US


Best regards,
Mike

entry.title: Can't load Data from Spaces - System down
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Are we the only ones reciving issues like "spaces couldn’t be loaded" since last system refresh  ( 16th Sun 24) ?

We can not reach the system / find view files or do any intervention.

entry.title: How to Improve Query Performance in SAP Datasphere when working with remote tables
entry.content:
Hello Experts

We have been working with SAP Datasphere, trying to recreate models previously built in MS SQL Server.

Because of that we chose as our main data product the SQL Views. The model consists of building SQL Views upon other SQL Views built upon Remote Tables.

Due to our company politics, we can only access data remotely, no data has to be stored in the cloud. Our data strategy is federation only.

But because of that, we have been having some issues. When previewing data, we pass the 3 minutes given by the platform and we can't preview.

I read this blog for advice on improving query performance: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/8-ways-to-increase-your-query-performance-in-sap-datasphere-when-federating/ba-p/13521723 

We tried running in analytical mode, that I believe is the same as activating the OLAP hint for SQL View (we tried with just one view) and the results are not satisfactory. We still can't preview data. Do we have to enable the analytical mode in all our views to see the improvement? (Our views are built upon views)

I was thinking maybe if we turn on "In-Memory Storage" for the remote tables we use to creating the SQL Views it could improve, but would that work for remote tables?

Replication is not an option for us. Our remote tables come from SAP HANA OP, integration via DPAgent and SDI.

Are there other ways to improve performance?

Thanks in advance

Rebeca

entry.title: How the data will comes from SAP Data sphere to SAP SCT?
entry.content:
Hey Folks, Greetings!

i.How the data is coming from SAP Data sphere to SAP SCT?

ii.How the data is coming from EM to Datasphere?

ii. Where we can store the SCT data's? Suppose, If is it SAP Datasphere mean How?

Pls Advise

Thanks

Tam

entry.title: PLM datasphere integration
entry.content: Are there any option to read PLM data into datasphere ? Are there any connectors available

entry.title: Bidirectional Cross Filtering in Datasphere
entry.content:
Dear experts, I created a graphical view based on ER-Model and connected tables with Joins. I would like to use a table as a filter and user cross filtering as it is possible in Power BI: Bidirectional cross-filtering in Power BI Desktop - Power BI | Microsoft Learn Unfortately I can not find any similar functionality. Can someone help me?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere OData Consumption API for Analytic Model
entry.content:
Dear community,

We are trying to consume the OData Endpoint of an analytic model. Because of Data Access Controls (DAC) applied to the underlying Fact View, we would also like to consume this data with an end users mail address/username. This way, based on the performer of the API Call, different data is pushed back.

When doing this in the browser, this works perfectly fine (see pictures).

However, we would like to use this data in a fiori application. For this, we want to automate the authorization flow but it seems like Datasphere is not accepting the token fetching in an automated way. Is there a way to login using client credentials OAuth?

Is this something related to the Client setup in Datasphere (Interactive/API Access?)

Kind regards,

Cas

entry.title: Not all data sources are available in the sources of Datasphere connections
entry.content:
Hello Datasphere experts,

I am trying to create a Data Flow in Datasphere but I can't because the tables I want to use from SAP HANA do not appear. When I select the Sources of my connection and look for the corresponding table (for example; NLEI) it does not appear neither in the list nor in the search engine.

I have tried typing the prefix, but also no prefix.

What am I missing to be able to use all SAP HANA tables in Datasphere?

thank you very much for your help!

entry.title: Re: Connection between SAP SAC and Datasphere
entry.content: Dear Experts i m also getting the same error when i try to access analytical view via sap analytics cloud i have  used same SAP Data sphere login details to login to sap analytics cloud but still getting the same error

entry.title: ABAP CDS extractor on MBV_MBEWH is not showing as Delta in Replication Flow (DataSphere)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have created a ABAP CDS delta extractor on MBV_MBEWH using CDC. SAP recommends to use MBV_MBEWH and not the table MBEWH directly. When the extractor is used in DataSphere Replication flow we do not get the option for delta.

Note - We are getting warning "Definition of view is too complex for automatic CDS" and "Data extraction of CDS entity not possible" in ABAP perspective. We ignored the warning as it was for other extractor's as well and they are fine.

Please let me know if we should create the delta extractor on table MBEWH directly? if not how can we resolve the issue.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: No entry in ODQMON and DHCDCMON after doing the Initial and Initial&Delta load in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are using replication flow in Datasphere to load data from S4HANA to Azure. We have observed that no entry is available in ODQMON  or DHCDCMON after doing the Initial and Initial&Delta load in Datasphere. Also the entries are not available in the /1DH/ML* and /1DH/SL* tables. The /1DH/OBSERVE_LOGTAB and /1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA jobs are scheduled and running fine in source.

The delta data is loaded in azure using the ABAP CDS extractors (example - C_PurchaseOrderDEX or C_PurchaseOrderItemDEX) and the data is available in Azure. 

Do we expect entry in ODQMON  or DHCDCMON? if yes why it is not showing up and what can be done to have these extractor entry.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Replication Flow, add remote source do not work without stopping the flow.
entry.content:
Hello,

Starting from version 2024.13 there is the possibility to add object to replication flow without resetting the delta for all objects.

But is SAP Help this option is not documented.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/da62e1ee746448e8bc043e1be4377cbe.html?locale=en-US

In Datasphere UI this possibility do not exists,

in a delta running replication flow, you can not add objects without resetting the delta for all objects!

Do you experience this behaviour also?

Thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: Not able to consume Datasphere objects in sac in datasphere Trial account
entry.content: Not able to consume Datasphere objects in sac in data sphere Trial account Created a Graphical model (analytical dataset) with consumption switch on.

entry.title: Date not grouped in story with Datasphere model
entry.content:
Hello SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere experts.

I have made an analytics model following this flow:

Replication Flow > Table > SQL View > Analytic Model

Within that process, I have a table that stores a field (ERDAT) as String since that is how I get it from the SAP table. In the SQL View I transform it into a date with the to_date() function. And this is done correctly, however, when I go to view it in the SAP Analytics Cloud history and I want to, for example, set a filter, they do not appear grouped as when I create the model directly in SAP Analytics Cloud:

 

but by individual dates:

I don't know how I can change this setting in SAP Datasphere. Or if it is something to change in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Best regards

entry.title: Why does my field rename not work in a view
entry.content:
Hello,

I've got a strange effect in a SQL View... The <<field>> AS "something" does not 'rename' the output column.

Any suggestions on what I'm doing wrong ?

entry.title: Why can't I select Hierarchy in the Preview
entry.content:
Hello,

Althought when looking in the Analytical model I can see that my dimension has a Hierarchy associated.

 

 

But in preview I can't select the hierarchy (For a very short while it worked) and must have changed something, bit doesn't apear anymore...

What could be wrong, everything is deployed ...

entry.title: NO RFC_AUTHORIZATION for functional module RPY_EXISTENCE_CHECK_FUNC in SAP BW Bridge
entry.content:
Hello,

  We are getting error which trying to perform a shell conversation of ADSO from on-premise BW 7.5 to SAP BW Bridge. The error message is " NO RFC Authorization for function module RPY_EXISTENCE_CHECK_FUNC". 

 

Appreciate your help.

Many thanks,

Balaji
